title: hook-manifest v1.0.0
description: Static integrity record for installed Tidereach hook scripts. Generated by hull's integrations/<cli>/ install tooling; consumed by interlock's verify-hooks (re-hash + compare) and hook-check (matcher fixture, cross-reference). The manifest is the integrity-input source for integrity-inputs/v1.0.0's hook_manifest_digest entry. See README.md for canonical serialization, pubkey use, and the separation from settings.json + audit-envelope hook_identity events.

manifest_version.description: Locked to the schema directory version. Consumers refuse mismatched values; bumping requires a contract minor or major release.

integration.description: Which agent CLI integration this manifest covers. Selects the per-integration adapter in hull's integrations/<cli>/ tree (hook_path conventions, matcher fixture location). Adding a new integration (e.g., cursor) is a v1.x minor bump.

hashes.description: Map of hook script path to SHA-256 lowercase hex digest. Paths are relative to the project root that registered the hook (NOT absolute, so manifests are portable across operator machines). verify-hooks re-hashes each script at the matching path and asserts the digest matches.

pubkey.description: Ed25519 public key (32 bytes, lowercase hex) used by interlock's verify-hooks to validate hook_identity audit-envelope events emitted from hook contexts. Null when no key is configured (hook_integrity_mode=off, or warn-only deployments). See README §Pubkey use for the runtime signature flow.

created_at.description: Epoch seconds UTC at which the manifest was generated. Used by audit tooling to correlate the manifest with installed-hook state at a point in time; the manifest is NOT timestamp-trust — script hashes are the integrity surface.
